page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c8a.12xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated physical cores utilizing the x86_64 architecture, running on an AMD EPYC 9T24 processor clocked at 2.7 GHz. The system is configured with 96.0 GB of RAM, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of memory per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 16 Gbps.

The hardware configuration delivers strong performance across multiple compute and memory benchmarks. In stress-ng testing, both single-core and multi-core CPU performance rank in the top 25 percent, supported by a high Passmark CPU score of 62,853.8. The processor's support for advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16, enables strong execution of vector and matrix operations. Memory performance is similarly robust, with cached small-block read bandwidth reaching 3,646.1 GB/sec and large-block write bandwidth measured at 83.95 GB/sec, though large-block read bandwidth drops to an average level of 113.72 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.c8a.12xlarge offers a high density of dedicated compute resources relative to its memory allocation, making it a viable option for CPU-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. The lack of bundled local storage and GPUs reduces overhead for deployments focused purely on processing power.

This instance is well-suited for high-throughput web serving, multi-threaded decompression tasks, and database operations. Benchmark results show static web serving throughput of 40.48 GB/sec and Redis SET performance of 64.43 million operations per second, confirming its suitability for high-concurrency web applications, caching layers, and parallel data processing pipelines.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c8a.12xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual server powered by an AMD EPYC 9T24 processor running at 2.7 GHz on the KVM hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated physical cores on the x86_64 architecture and 96.0 GB of RAM, providing a 1:2 core-to-memory ratio. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, but offers a baseline network bandwidth of 16 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates strong performance, with stress-ng CPU tests and Passmark scores ranking in the top 25 percent. The processor supports advanced vector and cryptography instructions, including AVX-512 and VAES. With a static web serving throughput of 40.48 GB/sec and Redis SET performance of 64.43 million operations per second, this instance is highly efficient for CPU-intensive workloads, database operations, multi-threaded decompression, and high-concurrency web serving.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c8a.12xlarge is a compute-optimized server with 48 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 96 GB RAM, and 16 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier CPU and database benchmark performance.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c8a.12xlarge is a compute-optimized server featuring 48 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 96 GB RAM, and 16 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A dedicated 48-core AMD EPYC compute-optimized instance delivering high-throughput processing and strong memory bandwidth for demanding workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated AMD EPYC 9T24 cores running at 2.7 GHz
96.0 GB of RAM providing a compute-optimized 1:2 resource ratio
16 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no bundled local storage
Strong CPU and database performance, including 64.43 million Redis SET operations per second
Advanced instruction support including AVX-512, AVX512_VNNI, and VAES
Optimized for high-concurrency web serving, database operations, and multi-threaded decompression
